things.name: Kvint Brandy Factory
things.description: World-renowned brandy distillery founded in 1897 producing Transnistria's most famous export; tours of the cellars with over 2 million bottles aging

things.name: Soviet Street Art and Murals
things.description: Tiraspol is a living Soviet time capsule with Lenin statues, Soviet-era murals and Cold War architecture unique in 21st-century Europe

things.name: Tiraspol History Museum
things.description: Regional museum with exhibits on the history of Transnistria, the 1992 war of independence and the cultural heritage of this unrecognised state

things.name: Bender Fortress
things.description: Enormous 16th-century Ottoman fortress on the Dniester river in Bender, one of the best-preserved Ottoman military structures in Eastern Europe

things.name: Central Park
things.description: Tiraspol's main Soviet-era park with a distinctive Ferris wheel, walking paths along the Dniester riverbank and public monuments of the Soviet period

things.name: Supreme Soviet Building
things.description: The parliament building of Transnistria, still displaying the hammer and sickle, Soviet flag and a Lenin statue; a unique piece of living Cold War history

things.name: Tiraspol Market
things.description: Bustling open-air market where locals shop for produce, Soviet memorabilia and Transnistrian goods; an authentic window into daily life in this enclave

things.name: Dniester River Beach
things.description: Sandy beaches along the Dniester river where locals swim and relax in summer; a surprisingly pleasant natural escape from the Soviet urban landscape

things.name: Chisinau (Day Trip)
things.description: Moldova's capital, 75km from Tiraspol, offers a wider range of museums, restaurants and the famous National Wine Festival each October

things.name: Sheriff Stadium
things.description: Home of FC Sheriff Tiraspol, backed by the powerful Sheriff conglomerate; memorably beat Real Madrid in the 2021 Champions League group stage
